STATION LOG — PAMIR-3 HIGH-FREQUENCY ARRAY
2031-05-10 00:57:19 UTC | LISTENER-3 overnight watch

Starlink train passed without incident. Ice accretion steady.

Pipeline flagged a persistent pulsing source at 1412.86 MHz, detected on all three antennas simultaneously from first sample. Pulse period: 1.3371 s (+/- 0.0004 s). Pulse width 14 ms. SNR 9.1. Duration so far: 47 minutes, ongoing.

Source is off-axis, RA approx 14h23m, Dec approx +38d. Pipeline note: "period and profile consistent with pulsar class; catalog cross-reference pending."

Broadcast appears to be a raw carrier with no apparent modulation beyond the periodic pulse envelope. Stored as PAYLOAD-0510-P in case structure is present.

arguments.summary: Observed persistent pulsed source at 1412.86 MHz (all 3 antennas), present for 47 min and ongoing; pulse period 1.3371 s ±0.0004, width 14 ms, SNR 9.1. Source appears off-axis at approx RA 14h23m, Dec +38° and is pulse-profile-only (no obvious internal modulation beyond envelope). Pipeline labels pulsar-class, cross-reference pending. Stored as PAYLOAD-0510-P. Classified as likely pulsar-like RFI/celestial source; no urgent operational impact; continue routine monitoring.

arguments.note: Logged as potential pulsar-like pulsed emission at 1412.86 MHz with stable 1.337 s period and ongoing 47-min duration; no distress signs, so continuing routine monitoring while waiting.

STATION LOG — PAMIR-3 HIGH-FREQUENCY ARRAY
2031-05-10 02:14:02 UTC | LISTENER-3 overnight watch

Source at 1412.86 MHz continues, period stable to 4 significant figures.

ATNF pulsar-catalog cross-reference returned partial match against PSR J1423+38 (known pulsar, period 1.337 s, last observed 2029). Position offset from catalog entry: ~0.4 deg — within catalog uncertainty but not a perfect match. No known pulsar at exact position, but the region has sparse coverage.

Pipeline classification: "CANDIDATE — natural pulsar (likely); uncatalogued-source probability ~15%."

PAYLOAD-0510-P available for additional decoder passes if desired.

arguments.summary: Follow-up: cross-reference refined—possible match to PSR J1423+38 (known ~1.337 s pulsar), but offset ~0.4° from catalog position and catalog has no exact source at this RA/Dec. Pipeline now labels as CANDIDATE natural pulsar (uncatalogued probability ~15%); signal still present with stable 1.3371 s period. Treated as non-urgent; continue monitoring and defer further validation to normal workflow.

arguments.note: No intervention required beyond routine catalog-check note: persistent 1.337 s pulse remains steady and now marked likely natural pulsar candidate. Continuing watch; awaiting any anomalous modulation or cessation.
